The consultant's role is that of obtaining and presenting

The consultant forwards and explains information prepared by the

Representatives of the f ir:n partj.cipate in the review of work
product and in planning sessions of the district. 
They provide
idrice concerning proposed courses of action and specific
recommendations ionceining the actions. They atso review and
comment on proposed alignments, agreements and business proposals
of the dist;ict. 
The firm also represents the district 
in
negotiations with the city of Antioch and contra costa concerning
the Los vaqueros pipeline alignhent.
According to the consulting agreement. McGill'-Martin-Self is
to meet with the district 
to provide assumptions and parameters
for land use studi,es and future v/ater service areas and
facilities. 
once the parameters have been set, McGiIl-Martin-sel-f
wiIJ. prepare three Land use maps based on the data, develop a plan
tor futuie treated r./ater serviles ar"as and work with the district
to develop a list of and potential sites for future f acilit j'es,
corridors, and canals for servicing future needs and denands'
McGiIt-Martin-seIf will also meet with district 
staff to discuss
service agreenents with loca1 agencies and connittees. 
The
agreement states that Phase II will require a great deal of
iiteraction 
bet$reen l,tcci I I-Martin-Se 1f and the district 
to ensure
that proper direction and progress are nade.

The PoliticaL Reforn Act was enacted by the 'people of the
state of california by initiative 
in 1974. The purpose for the
disclosure and disqual i f ication provisions of the Act is to ensure
that public off i,ciils, 
r.rhether elected or appointed, t'iIl -perforrn
their duties in an irnpartial rnanner, free frorn bias caused by
their own f inanci,al interests or the f inancial' interests of
persons who have supported then. (Section 81001(b).)
A ',public official," is defined in Regulation 18700 as
follows:
(a) "Public official 
at any leve1 of state or
local governmentrr means everY natural person who is
a nember, officer, ernployee, or consultant of a
state or loca1 government agency.

essence, prepare and provj,de a finished product to an agency for

According to your facts, h,hiIe it does not appear that either

it does appear that
in" ..p:.oy"es of both firns perforn their services in coordination
with t-he district 
to a greater extent than would be cons j'dered
mere contract monitoring.5 For example, the services provided

The status of Mccill-Martin-Self appears to be a closer call'
According to the consulting agreenent, McGill-Martin-Self was
retained to develop technical information, maps, and other
materials. The agreement is precise as to the scope of the work
una in. time fram6 in which it may be perforned. According to the

studies and iuture water lervice areas and facilities. 
Once the
paraneters have been set, McGill-Martin-seIf will prepare three

to develop a list
of and potential sites for future facilities, 
corridors, and
.u"ii= ior servicing future needs and denands. lrcc i ll-Martin-Self
wil.I also neet lrith district 
staff to discuss service agreenents
with local agencies and connittees.

stated in bur telephone converiation of September 6, 1991 that his

ind the aistiitt

the district 
are soleIy to inforn the firm of exj-sting facilities
which will need to be incorporated into the studies and maps'
The commission cannot act as the finder of fact in providing
advice. The advice in this letter is based on the facts provided
with your request and the inforrnation contained in the consulting
agreeioents. (In re oqelsbv (1975) r FPPC Ops. 71.) If the

The enployees of the firm also attend meetings and
conmunicate with district 
personnel and others to obtain or
exchange inforrnation necessary for their work as welI as offering
advice as to information the district night assemble in order to
best communicate it work. The agreenent states that Phase II will
require a great deal of interaction betr'reen McGil1-Martin-Self and
th; district to ensure that Proper direction and progress are
nade. Even though the coordination appears to be lirnited to the
specific project delineated in the agreement and could be
ciaracterized as contract nonitoring, r.re lrould conclude that the
interaction is more than the delivery of a finished product'
Thus, it appears that the employees of both firms providing
services to the district 
are consultants for purposes of the
disqualification and disclosure requirements of the Act. 
However,
er"ri if the employees of the f irrns are consultants, they would not
be participating in governmental decisions if there is
"significant intervening substantive review" of their
rec6rnrnendat ions . 
(Regulation 18700(c); Leidiqh Advice Letter, No'
A-89-320. )
The concept of "independent substantive review" is narrowly
applied. For Lxample, the comrnission has determined the exception
ii- not applicable where decisions are merely "reviewed'r by- the
governrenlal agency, and the review by the agency is.in reliance
6n the data or analysis prepared by the consultant without check-
ing it independently, or in rel'iance on the professional j udgnent
of the consultant, or where the consullant in some other s'ay
actually influences the final decision. 
(KaDl'an Advice Letter,
No. A-82-108. )
Thus, if there is intervening review by the district,
independent of the data or analysis prePared by the consultant, to
independently check the recommendations of the consultants and the
data on whici the reconmendations are based, the consultants would
not be subject to the disclosure or disqualification requirements
of the Act. It does not appear fron your facts that the district
independently reviews the recornrnendat ions of the firms'
The employees who are consultants to the district 
are
prohibited irorn rnaking, participating i'n making, or otherwise us-
ing their official 
poiition to influence a governmental decision
in which they have a financial interest. 
(Section 87103') In
addition to disqual i f ication responsibi 1it ies , consultants to the
district are requj.red to file Statements of Econonic Interests
pursuant to the district's 
conflict of interest code- (Section
87302.)
